title: Less than 5% of the population of low-income countries have the digital skills to use AI
subtitle: Population with digital skills, 2023
source: World Bank (2025), based on data from the International Telecommunication Union (ITU). Note: Basic digital skills refer to the basic use of digital devices, email communication, web search, and online transactions. Intermediate skills refer to the use of software and data management. Advanced skills refer to data analysis, software development, and high-level computing competences.
visdescription: Chart showing the share of population with basic, intermediate, and advanced digital skills, by income group.
prompt_text: Does everyone have the skills to use AI?
lessThanBasicSkillsTooltipText: [emphasis: Basic digital skills] refers to the basic use of digital devices, email communication, web search, and online transactions.
basicSkillsTooltipText: [emphasis: Basic digital skills] refers to the basic use of digital devices, email communication, web search, and online transactions.
intermediateSkillsTooltipText: [emphasis: Intermediate skills] refers to use of software and data management.
advancedSkillsTooltipText: [emphasis: Advanced skills] refers to data analysis, software development and high-level computing competences.
